name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: Pain is a gift from the gods, Lord Eddard,” Grand Maester Pycelle told him. “It means the bone is knitting, the flesh healing itself. Be thankful.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle把Eddard的pain称作gods gift，表示bone与flesh正在愈合。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pain is a gift把病人无法拒绝的痛苦重新命名为benefit；Pycelle说得医学上有部分道理，却忽略Eddard实际承受。
blocks.background_zh: **伤势：** Jaime伏击中horse压断Eddard leg；骨头正愈合但持续疼痛，milk of the poppy会镇痛并使意识昏沉。

blocks.original_text: “I will be thankful when my leg stops throbbing.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说只有腿不再throb时才会感恩。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle以Tywin来信试探Eddard，Eddard用Robert authority bluff回应；随后由Sansa童言完成Jon Arryn genealogy线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Pycelle set a stoppered flask on the table by the bed. “The milk of the poppy, for when the pain grows too onerous.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle留下stoppered flask，建议痛得难忍时用milk of the poppy。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle以Tywin来信试探Eddard，Eddard用Robert authority bluff回应；随后由Sansa童言完成Jon Arryn genealogy线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “I sleep too much already.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard拒绝，因为自己已经睡太多。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle以Tywin来信试探Eddard，Eddard用Robert authority bluff回应；随后由Sansa童言完成Jon Arryn genealogy线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Sleep is the great healer.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle称sleep是great healer。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle以Tywin来信试探Eddard，Eddard用Robert authority bluff回应；随后由Sansa童言完成Jon Arryn genealogy线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “I had hoped that was you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard讽刺自己原以为healer是Pycelle。
blocks.literary_note_zh: I had hoped that was you把great healer从sleep转回maester，讽刺Pycelle以格言替代有效止痛选择。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Pycelle smiled wanly. “It is good to see you in such a fierce humor, my lord.” He leaned close and lowered his voice. “There was a raven this morning, a letter for the queen from her lord father. I thought you had best know.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle勉强微笑后低声透露Tywin给Cersei来信，认为Eddard最好知道。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle先提供medicine再提供political message，两种‘照护’都可能服务Cersei influence。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Dark wings, dark words,” Ned said grimly. “What of it?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard以dark wings, dark words回应，问内容。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle以Tywin来信试探Eddard，Eddard用Robert authority bluff回应；随后由Sansa童言完成Jon Arryn genealogy线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Lord Tywin is greatly wroth about the men you sent after Ser Gregor Clegane,” the maester confided. “I feared he would be. You will recall, I said as much in council.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle说Tywin对派人追Gregor极为愤怒，并提醒自己早已在council警告。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle以Tywin来信试探Eddard，Eddard用Robert authority bluff回应；随后由Sansa童言完成Jon Arryn genealogy线索。
blocks.background_zh: **Tywin reaction：** Eddard已褫夺Gregor并派Beric持king banner执行；Tywin通过给Cersei来信表达愤怒。

blocks.original_text: “Let him be wroth,” Ned said. Every time his leg throbbed, he remembered Jaime Lannister’s smile, and Jory dead in his arms. “Let him write all the letters to the queen he likes. Lord Beric rides beneath the king’s own banner. If Lord Tywin attempts to interfere with the king’s justice, he will have Robert to answer to. The only thing His Grace enjoys more than hunting is making war on lords who defy him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard让Tywin尽管生气，强调Beric持king banner，若阻碍justice便要面对好战的Robert。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Robert好战威慑Tywin，却因下一段承认并不自信，显示这是strategic bluff而非稳定依靠。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Pycelle pulled back, his maester’s chain jangling. “As you say. I shall visit again on the morrow.” The old man hurriedly gathered up his things and took his leave. Ned had little doubt that he was bound straight for the royal apartments, to whisper at the queen. I thought you had best know, indeed . . . as if Cersei had not instructed him to pass along her father’s threats. He hoped his response rattled those perfect teeth of hers. Ned was not near as confident of Robert as he pretended, but there was no reason Cersei need know that.
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle匆忙离开；Eddard判断他会向Cersei汇报，自己对Robert支持其实远没有表演得自信。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他同时读出message与meta-message：Pycelle不是单纯告知，而在传递Cersei/Tywin的threat并收集回应。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: When Pycelle was gone, Ned called for a cup of honeyed wine. That clouded the mind as well, yet not as badly. He needed to be able to think. A thousand times, he asked himself what Jon Arryn might have done, had he lived long enough to act on what he’d learned. Or perhaps he had acted, and died for it.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard改喝较轻微影响思考的honeyed wine，反复想Jon Arryn若活着会如何行动，或是否已行动而死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: honeyed wine也cloud mind但较轻，表明Eddard在pain management与cognitive clarity间做风险权衡。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: It was queer how sometimes a child’s innocent eyes can see things that grown men are blind to. Someday, when Sansa was grown, he would have to tell her how she had made it all come clear for him. He’s not the least bit like that old drunken king, she had declared, angry and unknowing, and the simple truth of it had twisted inside him, cold as death. This was the sword that killed Jon Arryn, Ned thought then, and it will kill Robert as well, a slower death but full as certain. Shattered legs may heal in time, but some betrayals fester and poison the soul.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他意识Sansa无意说出Joffrey不像Robert，使全部线索清晰；这truth杀了Jon，也将以背叛毒害Robert。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Sansa愤怒童言绕过成人假设；sword that killed Jon是metaphor，真正致命者仍可能是为保守truth采取行动的人。
blocks.background_zh: **Sansa clue：** 上章Sansa说Joffrey不像Robert，Arya指出他法律上应是stag而非lion；Eddard将此与genealogy book连接。

blocks.original_text: Littlefinger came calling an hour after the Grand Maester had left, clad in a plum-colored doublet with a mockingbird embroidered on the breast in black thread, and a striped cloak of black and white. “I cannot visit long, my lord,” he announced. “Lady Tanda expects me to lunch with her. No doubt she will roast me a fatted calf. If it’s near as fatted as her daughter, I’m like to rupture and die. And how is your leg?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger盛装来访，拿Tanda daughter体形开恶毒玩笑后问腿伤。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger拿Lollys身体作笑料，展示其wit经常依赖羞辱更弱势者；这也提醒读者不要把聪明自动等同可靠。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Inflamed and painful, with an itch that is driving me mad.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说伤处发炎疼痛且itch难忍。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger带来west mobilization与hunt消息，试探genealogy book后离开；Eddard意识daughters三日后启程，必须尽快行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Littlefinger lifted an eyebrow. “In future, try not to let any horses fall on it. I would urge you to heal quickly. The realm grows restive. Varys has heard ominous whispers from the west. Freeriders and sellswords have been flocking to Casterly Rock, and not for the thin pleasure of Lord Tywin’s conversation.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger玩笑别再让horse压腿，随后报告west ominous：freeriders、sellswords正在Casterly Rock聚集。
blocks.literary_note_zh: freeriders/sellswords flocking是war mobilization indicator，但Varys whispers和Littlefinger转述均非正式军令证据。
blocks.background_zh: **west mobilization：** Casterly Rock招集freeriders、sellswords通常意味着扩军，但消息来自Varys whispers经Littlefinger转述。

blocks.original_text: “Is there word of the king?” Ned demanded. “Just how long does Robert intend to hunt?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard急问Robert还要hunt多久。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger带来west mobilization与hunt消息，试探genealogy book后离开；Eddard意识daughters三日后启程，必须尽快行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Given his preferences, I believe he’d stay in the forest until you and the queen both die of old age,” Lord Petyr replied with a faint smile. “Lacking that, I imagine he’ll return as soon as he’s killed something. They found the white hart, it seems . . . or rather, what remained of it. Some wolves found it first, and left His Grace scarcely more than a hoof and a horn. Robert was in a fury, until he heard talk of some monstrous boar deeper in the forest. Then nothing would do but he must have it. Prince Joffrey returned this morning, with the Royces, Ser Balon Swann, and some twenty others of the party. The rest are still with the king.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger说wolves先吃了white hart，Robert改追monstrous boar；Joffrey等部分队伍已返城。
blocks.literary_note_zh: white hart magic quest被wolves先行终结，Robert立刻用boar替换目标；hunt欲望永远能找到新对象。
blocks.background_zh: **hunt状态：** white hart已被wolves吃，Robert转追boar；Joffrey、Hound等部分人已返King’s Landing。

blocks.original_text: “The Hound?” Ned asked, frowning. Of all the Lannister party, Sandor Clegane was the one who concerned him the most, now that Ser Jaime had fled the city to join his father.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard最关心Hound是否回来，因为Jaime离开后他是城内Lannister一方最危险人物。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger带来west mobilization与hunt消息，试探genealogy book后离开；Eddard意识daughters三日后启程，必须尽快行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, returned with Joffrey, and went straight to the queen.” Littlefinger smiled. “I would have given a hundred silver stags to have been a roach in the rushes when he learned that Lord Beric was off to behead his brother.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger说Hound随Joffrey直奔queen，并想偷听他得知Beric去杀Gregor时的反应。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger带来west mobilization与hunt消息，试探genealogy book后离开；Eddard意识daughters三日后启程，必须尽快行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Even a blind man could see the Hound loathed his brother.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard指出Hound本就厌恶brother。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger带来west mobilization与hunt消息，试探genealogy book后离开；Eddard意识daughters三日后启程，必须尽快行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Ah, but Gregor was his to loathe, not yours to kill. Once Dondarrion lops the summit off our Mountain, the Clegane lands and incomes will pass to Sandor, but I wouldn’t hold my water waiting for his thanks, not that one. And now you must forgive me. Lady Tanda awaits with her fatted calves.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger说Gregor只可由Sandor恨，不可由别人杀；Gregor死后land、income将归Sandor，却别期待感谢。
blocks.literary_note_zh: his to loathe指出家庭仇恨也被Sandor体验为private possession；外人杀Gregor可能剥夺其想象中的清算权。
blocks.background_zh: **Clegane inheritance：** 若Gregor依法死亡且无其他继承障碍，其lands、income可能转给brother Sandor。

blocks.original_text: On the way to the door, Lord Petyr spied Grand Maester Malleon’s massive tome on the table and paused to idly flip open the cover. “The Lineages and Histories of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms, With Descriptions of Many High Lords and Noble Ladies and Their Children,“ he read. “Now there is tedious reading if ever I saw it. A sleeping potion, my lord?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger临走看见Malleon genealogical tome，念出长title并嘲为sleeping potion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 书名本身就是证据目录：lineages、lords、ladies、children把政治秘密藏在tedious genealogy中。源页末引号方向异常按原文保留。
blocks.background_zh: **Malleon tome：** 记录great houses婚配、children与外貌；Jon Arryn、Eddard均用它检查Baratheon–Lannister血统规律。

blocks.original_text: For a brief moment Ned considered telling him all of it, but there was something in Littlefinger’s japes that irked him. The man was too clever by half, a mocking smile never far from his lips. “Jon Arryn was studying this volume when he was taken sick,” Ned said in a careful tone, to see how he might respond.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard短暂想全盘告诉他，却因其过度聪明和japes不信任，只试探说Jon Arryn病前在读此书。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard用Jon读过此书测试Littlefinger反应，却只得到joke；无反应既不证明无知，也不证明掩饰。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: And he responded as he always did: with a quip. “In that case,” he said, “death must have come as a blessed relief.” Lord Petyr Baelish bowed and took his leave.
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger用死亡终于解脱的quip回避，鞠躬离开。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger带来west mobilization与hunt消息，试探genealogy book后离开；Eddard意识daughters三日后启程，必须尽快行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Eddard Stark allowed himself a curse. Aside from his own retainers, there was scarcely a man in this city he trusted. Littlefinger had concealed Catelyn and helped Ned in his inquiries, yet his haste to save his own skin when Jaime and his swords had come out of the rain still rankled. Varys was worse. For all his protestations of loyalty, the eunuch knew too much and did too little. Grand Maester Pycelle seemed more Cersei’s creature with every passing day, and Ser Barristan was an old man, and rigid. He would tell Ned to do his duty.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard咒骂city无人可信：Littlefinger会自保，Varys知而不做，Pycelle愈像Cersei的人，Barristan又僵守duty。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 对四人的评估暴露Eddard困境：他需要帮助，却用忠诚、行动、rigidity标准逐个找到不能完全信任的理由。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Time was perilously short. The king would return from his hunt soon, and honor would require Ned to go to him with all he had learned. Vayon Poole had arranged for Sansa and Arya to sail on the Wind Witch out of Braavos, three days hence. They would be back at Winterfell before the harvest. Ned could no longer use his concern for their safety to excuse his delay.
blocks.explanation_zh: 时间紧迫；Robert将返、honor要求报告，而Sansa、Arya三日后乘Wind Witch离开，Eddard不能再以安全为拖延理由。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Wind Witch和three days hence给道德选择设倒计时；daughters安全不再是无限延期理由。
blocks.background_zh: **Wind Witch：** 从Braavos来的ship，Poole安排三日后载Sansa、Arya秘密北返，预计harvest前到Winterfell。

blocks.original_text: Yet last night he had dreamt of Rhaegar’s children. Lord Tywin had laid the bodies beneath the Iron Throne, wrapped in the crimson cloaks of his house guard. That was clever of him; the blood did not show so badly against the red cloth. The little princess had been barefoot, still dressed in her bed gown, and the boy . . . the boy . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: 但他梦见Tywin曾把Rhaegar两名child bodies裹Lannister red cloaks放在Iron Throne下。
blocks.literary_note_zh: red cloaks掩blood的图像把Tywin cleverness写成美化证据，而非减少罪行。
blocks.background_zh: **Rhaegar children：** Rebellion末期Tywin forces杀死年幼princess与prince，并以red Lannister cloaks包裹尸体献给Robert。

blocks.original_text: Ned could not let that happen again. The realm could not withstand a second mad king, another dance of blood and vengeance. He must find some way to save the children.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard不能让children massacre重演，也不能让realm再陷mad king与blood vengeance。
blocks.literary_note_zh: save the children来自旧日失败记忆；Eddard把当前三个child与Rhaegar children放进同一保护伦理。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Robert could be merciful. Ser Barristan was scarcely the only man he had pardoned. Grand Maester Pycelle, Varys the Spider, Lord Balon Greyjoy; each had been counted an enemy to Robert once, and each had been welcomed into friendship and allowed to retain honors and office for a pledge of fealty. So long as a man was brave and honest, Robert would treat him with all the honor and respect due a valiant enemy.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他回顾Robert曾宽恕Barristan、Pycelle、Varys、Balon等valiant former enemies，试图寻找mercy可能。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert能尊重公开勇敌，因为战争身份清楚；这并不能直接推出他会宽恕家庭与继承背叛。
blocks.background_zh: **Robert pardons：** Barristan等原敌在宣誓fealty后获保留honor或office，说明Robert对公开战场敌人可宽容。

blocks.original_text: This was something else: poison in the dark, a knife thrust to the soul. This he could never forgive, no more than he had forgiven Rhaegar. He will kill them all, Ned realized.
blocks.explanation_zh: 但秘密背叛像poison与刺入soul的knife，Robert绝不会像宽恕公开敌人般宽恕，必杀children。
blocks.literary_note_zh: poison in dark回扣Jon疑似poison与secret parentage；Robert把private betrayal体验为比battle更不可原谅。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: And yet, he knew he could not keep silent. He had a duty to Robert, to the realm, to the shade of Jon Arryn . . . and to Bran, who surely must have stumbled on some part of the truth. Why else would they have tried to slay him?
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard仍不能沉默，他对Robert、realm、Jon Arryn与Bran均有duty；Bran遇害动机也与truth有关。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard把Bran attempted murder纳入motive chain：发现秘密可解释灭口企图，但尚未直接证明谁执行push或dagger attack。
blocks.background_zh: **Bran link：** Bran fall后另有assassin持dagger灭口；Eddard现推断Bran可能先发现royal children秘密。

blocks.original_text: Late that afternoon he summoned Tomard, the portly guardsman with the ginger-colored whiskers his children called Fat Tom. With Jory dead and Alyn gone, Fat Tom had command of his household guard. The thought filled Ned with vague disquiet. Tomard was a solid man; affable, loyal, tireless, capable in a limited way, but he was near fifty, and even in his youth he had never been energetic. Perhaps Ned should not have been so quick to send off half his guard, and all his best swords among them.
blocks.explanation_zh: 下午Eddard召Fat Tom；Jory死、Alyn离后由其领household guard，使Eddard因兵少与能力有限而不安。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 先前把best swords派给Beric如今反噬本地安全；正确执行一项justice也可能削弱另一项保护任务。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “I shall require your help,” Ned said when Tomard appeared, looking faintly apprehensive, as he always did when called before his lord. “Take me to the godswood.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说需要Tomard帮助，命带自己去godswood。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。

blocks.original_text: “Is that wise, Lord Eddard? With your leg and all?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tomard怀疑伤腿外出是否明智。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。

blocks.original_text: “Perhaps not. But necessary.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard承认或许不明智但necessary。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Perhaps not. But necessary压缩Eddard moral style：承认手段危险，不因此放弃认为必须的行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Tomard summoned Varly. With one arm around each man’s shoulders, Ned managed to descend the steep tower steps and hobble across the bailey. “I want the guard doubled,” he told Fat Tom. “No one enters or leaves the Tower of the Hand without my leave.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tomard与Varly架Eddard下楼过bailey；Eddard命Tower guard加倍，出入需许可。
blocks.literary_note_zh: double guard与controlled access说明他知道对质后风险上升，却仍因人手不足只能lengthen watches。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Tom blinked. “M’lord, with Alyn and the others away, we are hard-pressed already—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tomard说Alyn等走后人手已经紧张。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “It will only be a short while. Lengthen the watches.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard命延长watch，称只需短时间。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “As you say, m’lord,” Tom answered. “Might I ask why—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tomard服从但想问原因。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Best not,” Ned answered crisply.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard简短阻止其知道更多。
blocks.literary_note_zh: need-to-know保护Tomard免受秘密牵连，也剥夺guard commander理解威胁、主动调整的能力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: The godswood was empty, as it always was here in this citadel of the southron gods. Ned’s leg was screaming as they lowered him to the grass beside the heart tree. “Thank you.” He drew a paper from his sleeve, sealed with the sigil of his House. “Kindly deliver this at once.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 南方godswood空无一人；Eddard在faceless oak旁坐下，交Tomard一封Stark-sealed paper立即送出。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。
blocks.background_zh: **godswood地点：** Red Keep godswood无weirwood，heart tree为faceless oak；隐蔽安静，适合不被court听见的对质。

blocks.original_text: Tomard looked at the name Ned had written on the paper and licked his lips anxiously. “My lord . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tomard看到收件人后焦虑舔唇。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Do as I bid you, Tom,” Ned said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard命他照办。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children梦令Eddard决心避免新屠杀；他加强Tower守卫、秘密送信，并带伤到godswood等待Cersei。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: How long he waited in the quiet of the godswood, he could not say. It was peaceful here. The thick walls shut out the clamor of the castle, and he could hear birds singing, the murmur of crickets, leaves rustling in a gentle wind. The heart tree was an oak, brown and faceless, yet Ned Stark still felt the presence of his gods. His leg did not seem to hurt so much.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard在隔绝castle噪声的godswood等待，听birds、crickets、leaves；虽heart tree为oak，仍感到gods与疼痛减轻。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 无weirwood face的oak仍让Eddard感到gods，说明faith不完全依赖正统物质标志；环境平静也降低pain salience。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: She came to him at sunset, as the clouds reddened above the walls and towers. She came alone, as he had bid her. For once she was dressed simply, in leather boots and hunting greens. When she drew back the hood of her brown cloak, he saw the bruise where the king had struck her. The angry plum color had faded to yellow, and the swelling was down, but there was no mistaking it for anything but what it was.
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei sunset时按要求独自到来，穿简朴hunting greens，脸上仍有Robert打出的yellow bruise。
blocks.literary_note_zh: yellow bruise把上一场bedchamber violence带入本次秘密会面；simple clothes不消除queen power。
blocks.background_zh: **bruise：** Robert此前在Eddard bedchamber争吵中反手打倒Cersei；伤痕仍清晰可辨。

blocks.original_text: “Why here?” Cersei Lannister asked as she stood over him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei站在Eddard上方问为何选这里。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人在oak下对质：Cersei承认Jaime关系、Bran看见秘密、三名children paternity；Eddard陈述black-hair genealogy证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “So the gods can see.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说为了让gods看见。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 让gods see表明Eddard把对质当truth oath场，而非纯政治交易。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: She sat beside him on the grass. Her every move was graceful. Her curling blond hair moved in the wind, and her eyes were green as the leaves of summer. It had been a long time since Ned Stark had seen her beauty, but he saw it now. “I know the truth Jon Arryn died for,” he told her.
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei坐草地仍优雅美丽；Eddard直说自己知道Jon Arryn因何而死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他重新看见beauty却仍提出指控，避免把moral judgment写成对外貌厌恶。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Do you?” The queen watched his face, wary as a cat. “Is that why you called me here, Lord Stark? To pose me riddles? Or is it your intent to seize me, as your wife seized my brother?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei警惕观察，问这是riddle还是要像Catelyn抓Tyrion般抓她。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人在oak下对质：Cersei承认Jaime关系、Bran看见秘密、三名children paternity；Eddard陈述black-hair genealogy证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “If you truly believed that, you would never have come.” Ned touched her cheek gently. “Has he done this before?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard判断若真怕被捕她不会来，轻触bruise问Robert是否曾多次施暴。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard未经明确同意触碰Cersei伤处附近，Cersei随即shy away；关怀意图不取消身体边界。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Once or twice.” She shied away from his hand. “Never on the face before. Jaime would have killed him, even if it meant his own life.” Cersei looked at him defiantly. “My brother is worth a hundred of your friend.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei躲开，说此前一两次但从未打脸，并宣称Jaime会为此杀Robert，远胜Eddard friend。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei用Jaime willingness to kill证明价值，把保护与致命暴力紧密绑定。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Your brother?” Ned said. “Or your lover?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard直接问Jaime是brother还是lover。
blocks.literary_note_zh: brother or lover以二选一逼出秘密，Cersei用both拒绝社会分类边界。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: [原文中Cersei确认Jaime同时是brother与lover，将关系追溯至childhood，并以Targaryen近亲婚配及双生一体为辩护；因含露骨性表达，此处不逐字复录。请参见源 PDF p.446 对应段落。]
blocks.explanation_zh: 本段中Cersei确认Jaime同时是brother与lover，称关系源自childhood，并以Targaryen婚配与双生一体为辩护；露骨句不复录。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段含露骨incest表达并追溯到childhood，不逐字复录。核心是Cersei把禁忌关系解释为Targaryen precedent与twin wholeness。
blocks.background_zh: **安全说明：** 本段含露骨incest表达并将关系追溯至childhood；保留承认、辩护逻辑和定位，不逐字复录。

blocks.original_text: “My son Bran . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard提起son Bran。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人在oak下对质：Cersei承认Jaime关系、Bran看见秘密、三名children paternity；Eddard陈述black-hair genealogy证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: To her credit, Cersei did not look away. “He saw us. You love your children, do you not?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei不回避，说Bran看见了，并反问Eddard是否爱children。
blocks.literary_note_zh: He saw us确认Bran发现incest，为坠塔提供motive；Cersei没有在此直接说谁推他。
blocks.background_zh: **证据边界：** Cersei承认Bran看见两人；这确立灭口motive，不等于她在此承认亲手推落或派dagger assassin。

blocks.original_text: Robert had asked him the very same question, the morning of the melee. He gave her the same answer. “With all my heart.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard像Robert此前问他时一样回答全心爱。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人在oak下对质：Cersei承认Jaime关系、Bran看见秘密、三名children paternity；Eddard陈述black-hair genealogy证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “No less do I love mine.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei说自己同样爱children。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人在oak下对质：Cersei承认Jaime关系、Bran看见秘密、三名children paternity；Eddard陈述black-hair genealogy证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Ned thought, If it came to that, the life of some child I did not know, against Robb and Sansa and Arya and Bran and Rickon, what would I do? Even more so, what would Catelyn do, if it were Jon’s life, against the children of her body? He did not know. He prayed he never would.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard设想若陌生child与自己五个children冲突，甚至Jon与Catelyn亲生孩子冲突，自己也不知会如何选。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard不轻易以自己道德优越否认parental dilemma；这种同理使他随后给Cersei children逃生机会。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “All three are Jaime’s,” he said. It was not a question.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard断言Cersei三个children全由Jaime所生。
blocks.literary_note_zh: not a question表明genealogy已让Eddard形成结论，本次对话是confirmation与安排后果，不是开放调查。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Thank the gods.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei回答thank the gods，确认而非否认。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Thank the gods不仅承认paternity，也公开表达她不愿Robert成为children father。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: The seed is strong, Jon Arryn had cried on his deathbed, and so it was. All those bastards, all with hair as black as night. Grand Maester Malleon recorded the last mating between stag and lion, some ninety years ago, when Tya Lannister wed Gowen Baratheon, third son of the reigning lord. Their only issue, an unnamed boy described in Malleon’s tome as a large and lusty lad born with a full head of black hair, died in infancy. Thirty years before that a male Lannister had taken a Baratheon maid to wife. She had given him three daughters and a son, each black-haired. No matter how far back Ned searched in the brittle yellowed pages, always he found the gold yielding before the coal.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard串起seed is strong：Robert bastards与历代stag–lion children均黑发，记录中gold总向coal屈服。
blocks.literary_note_zh: gold yielding before coal是书中多代observational pattern，在in-world推理中强；现代遗传上不能把发色当绝对法则，但Cersei confession补足事实。
blocks.background_zh: **genealogy chain：** Robert多个bastards黑发；书中多次stag–lion marriage children也黑发；Joffrey、Myrcella、Tommen均gold hair；Cersei随后确认Jaime paternity。

blocks.original_text: “A dozen years,” Ned said. “How is it that you have had no children by the king?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问十二年婚姻为何没有Robert children。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人在oak下对质：Cersei承认Jaime关系、Bran看见秘密、三名children paternity；Eddard陈述black-hair genealogy证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: She lifted her head, defiant. “Your Robert got me with child once,” she said, her voice thick with contempt. “My brother found a woman to cleanse me. He never knew. If truth be told, I can scarcely bear for him to touch me, and I have not let him inside me for years. I know other ways to pleasure him, when he leaves his whores long enough to stagger up to my bedchamber. Whatever we do, the king is usually so drunk that he’s forgotten it all by the next morning.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei说曾怀Robert child但秘密终止，此后通过避免性交与利用其醉酒维持表象。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段涉及成人生育终止与婚内性安排；以reproductive control与长期婚姻欺骗分析，不道德化医疗选择本身。
blocks.background_zh: **成人生育叙事：** Cersei描述曾终止Robert pregnancy并长期控制性接触；本段所有当事人为成年人。

blocks.original_text: How could they have all been so blind? The truth was there in front of them all the time, written on the children’s faces. Ned felt sick. “I remember Robert as he was the day he took the throne, every inch a king,” he said quietly. “A thousand other women might have loved him with all their hearts. What did he do to make you hate him so?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard震惊众人长久未见children脸上的truth，又追问Robert当年像king，究竟如何令她憎恨。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard怀念年轻Robert every inch a king，说明他仍用过去潜力衡量现在person，追问hate原因也寻找道德对称。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: Her eyes burned, green fire in the dusk, like the lioness that was her sigil. “The night of our wedding feast, the first time we shared a bed, he called me by your sister’s name. He was on top of me, in me, stinking of wine, and he whispered Lyanna.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei说婚礼夜第一次同床，醉酒Robert在亲密时叫她Lyanna。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert说Lyanna的名字把Cersei在婚姻开端降为替代者；酒味与错名形成持久羞辱，但不为后续伤害无条件免责。
blocks.background_zh: **Lyanna shadow：** Robert仍执着Eddard sister Lyanna；婚礼夜错名使其旧爱直接进入Cersei婚姻。

blocks.original_text: Ned Stark thought of pale blue roses, and for a moment he wanted to weep. “I do not know which of you I pity most.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard想到pale blue roses，悲伤说不知更该怜悯夫妻哪一个。
blocks.literary_note_zh: pale blue roses连接Lyanna记忆；pity both承认婚姻双方被一个亡者形象困住。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: The queen seemed amused by that. “Save your pity for yourself, Lord Stark. I want none of it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei拒绝pity，叫他留给自己。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei解释与Robert婚姻、试图诱Eddard结盟；遭拒后攻击其honor，Eddard给她带children逃亡窗口，双方以game of thrones分歧结束。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “You know what I must do.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说她知道自己必须做什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei解释与Robert婚姻、试图诱Eddard结盟；遭拒后攻击其honor，Eddard给她带children逃亡窗口，双方以game of thrones分歧结束。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Must?” She put her hand on his good leg, just above the knee. “A true man does what he will, not what he must.” Her fingers brushed lightly against his thigh, the gentlest of promises. “The realm needs a strong Hand. Joff will not come of age for years. No one wants war again, least of all me.” Her hand touched his face, his hair. “If friends can turn to enemies, enemies can become friends. Your wife is a thousand leagues away, and my brother has fled. Be kind to me, Ned. I swear to you, you shall never regret it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei以will高于must诱惑Eddard结盟，触摸其腿、脸与头发，承诺强Hand可避免war且令他不后悔。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei从法律truth转向body、power与war avoidance交易；真正offer是共同隐瞒以换political partnership。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Did you make the same offer to Jon Arryn?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问她是否也向Jon Arryn作过同样offer。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 问题暗含怀疑：若Jon也收到offer却死亡，Cersei是否与其死有关；文本在此仍未得到直接回答。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: She slapped him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei扇他一耳光。
blocks.literary_note_zh: slap终止seduction frame并恢复敌对；身体动作比言辞更快回应Jon暗示。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “I shall wear that as a badge of honor,” Ned said dryly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard借Cersei曾说的badge of honor干讽回应。
blocks.literary_note_zh: badge of honor回用Cersei被Robert打后说过的话，机智却也把两次性质不同的掌掴并列。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Honor,” she spat. “How dare you play the noble lord with me! What do you take me for? You’ve a bastard of your own, I’ve seen him. Who was the mother, I wonder? Some Dornish peasant you raped while her holdfast burned? A whore? Or was it the grieving sister, the Lady Ashara? She threw herself into the sea, I’m told. Why was that? For the brother you slew, or the child you stole? Tell me, my honorable Lord Eddard, how are you any different from Robert, or me, or Jaime?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei攻击Eddard私生子与Ashara等传闻，质问他凭何以noble lord自居、与Robert三人何异。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei用Jon Snow攻击Eddard hypocrisy，并引Ashara rumors；这些都是指控与传闻，本段不证明mother身份或事件真相。
blocks.background_zh: **Ashara rumor：** Cersei列举Dornish peasant、whore、Ashara等Jon mother传闻；本章没有确认任何版本。

blocks.original_text: “For a start,” said Ned, “I do not kill children. You would do well to listen, my lady. I shall say this only once. When the king returns from his hunt, I intend to lay the truth before him. You must be gone by then. You and your children, all three, and not to Casterly Rock. If I were you, I should take ship for the Free Cities, or even farther, to the Summer Isles or the Port of Ibben. As far as the winds blow.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard首先区分自己不杀children，宣布Robert返城即告知truth，给Cersei与三名children时间逃到远方Free Cities。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard先说不杀children，揭示他认为最关键差别不是sexual purity而是对无辜者的暴力底线。
blocks.background_zh: **escape window：** Eddard不先逮捕，而打算Robert返前让Cersei携children远逃，以避免children被处死。

blocks.original_text: “Exile,” she said. “A bitter cup to drink from.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei称exile是bitter cup。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei解释与Robert婚姻、试图诱Eddard结盟；遭拒后攻击其honor，Eddard给她带children逃亡窗口，双方以game of thrones分歧结束。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “A sweeter cup than your father served Rhaegar’s children,” Ned said, “and kinder than you deserve. Your father and your brothers would do well to go with you. Lord Tywin’s gold will buy you comfort and hire swords to keep you safe. You shall need them. I promise you, no matter where you flee, Robert’s wrath will follow you, to the back of beyond if need be.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说仍比Tywin给Rhaegar children的结局甜，建议Tywin、Jaime等同逃，并警告Robert wrath会追到极远。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他给出的不是legal acquittal而是forewarning；若Cersei不走，Eddard仍会告诉Robert并触发追杀。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: The queen stood. “And what of my wrath, Lord Stark?” she asked softly. Her eyes searched his face. “You should have taken the realm for yourself. It was there for the taking. Jaime told me how you found him on the Iron Throne the day King’s Landing fell, and made him yield it up. That was your moment. All you needed to do was climb those steps, and sit. Such a sad mistake.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei起身反问自己的wrath，称Eddard在King’s Landing陷落时本可从Jaime手中取Iron Throne，却错失掌权时刻。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei把不夺throne定义为mistake，暴露其政治尺度：未取得power即浪费机会，合法与否居次。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “I have made more mistakes than you can possibly imagine,” Ned said, “but that was not one of them.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard承认犯过许多错，但不夺throne不是其中之一。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Cersei解释与Robert婚姻、试图诱Eddard结盟；遭拒后攻击其honor，Eddard给她带children逃亡窗口，双方以game of thrones分歧结束。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, but it was, my lord,” Cersei insisted. “When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die. There is no middle ground.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Cersei给出game of thrones规则：只能win或die，没有middle ground。
blocks.literary_note_zh: win or die是Cersei worldview，不是中立宇宙定律；它会使任何middle-ground mercy看起来像致命软弱。
blocks.background_zh: **game of thrones：** Cersei用来概括高位权力斗争的生死零和观；Eddard仍试图创造exile middle ground。

blocks.original_text: She turned up her hood to hide her swollen face and left him there in the dark beneath the oak, amidst the quiet of the godswood, under a blue-black sky. The stars were coming out.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她戴hood遮住肿脸离去，把Eddard独留blue-black夜色、oak与初升stars下。
blocks.literary_note_zh: hood同时遮bruise与身份脆弱，Eddard留在faceless oak下；双方都离开对质却没有共同可执行协议。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在genealogy证据、Cersei confession、Eddard duty与children safety的冲突。
